articles.title: Meta in talks to rent some of its billions in AI infrastructure to Anthropic
articles.description: By Clare Duffy, CNN New York (CNN) — Meta is in talks with Anthropic about leasing computing capacity to the AI startup. articles.title: SpaceX might land another big compute customer: the Department of Defense.
articles.description:
The Pentagon may make a deal where it would pay for billions of dollars of compute from SpaceX, according to The Wall Street Journal.
SpaceX already provides compute to Google and Anthropic, and the company wants to do more in cloud computing, the WSJ says.
[Link: SpaceX in Talks to Provide Computing Power for Pentagon’s AI Push | https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/spacex-in-talks-to-provide-computing-power-for-pentagons-ai-push-15e752e4?mod=rss_Technology | The Wall Street Journal]

articles.title: MLB restricts dugout iPads to bar use of AI to make game decisions
articles.description: In some cases, use of the dugout iPad grew to include recommendations regarding substitutions, pitch calling, and other in-game decisions traditionally made by players and coaches.
articles.content:
NEW YORK — Major League Baseball is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent the tablets from running artificial intelligence to help make decisions.

articles.title: China's Kimi K3 rattles US AI industry
articles.description: WASHINGTON — Kimi K3, a new artificial intelligence program from a Chinese startup called Moonshot AI, stunned the US tech industry on Friday, setting off fresh discussion over the China-US rivalry to dominate AI.
articles.content:
WASHINGTON — Kimi K3, a new artificial intelligence program from a Chinese startup called Moonshot AI, stunned the US tech industry on Friday, setting off fresh discussion over the China-US rivalry to dominate AI.

articles.title: The sell-off for AI stars worsens, while oil prices keep jumping
articles.description: The sell-off for AI winners deepened and yanked stock markets lower worldwide. The S&P 500 fell 1% Friday to finish its first losing week in the last three and only its third since March. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 0.8%, and the Nasdaq composite sank 1.4%. Nvidia was the heaviest weight on the market and briefly gave up its status as the most valuable stock on Wall Street. The worries about whether AI stocks have shot too high dragged down Asian markets even more sharply. Oil prices jumped again on worries about the war with Iran, but longer-term Treasury yields eased.
articles.content:
NEW YORK (AP) — The sell-off for winners of the artificial-intelligence boom deepened Friday and yanked stock markets lower worldwide. Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to jump because of the war with Iran.
The S&P 500 fell 1% to finish its first losi... [1465 chars]

articles.title: Netflix Paid $587 Million for Ben Affleck’s AI Company
articles.description: Netflix bought Ben Affleck's AI company for $587 million, new filing reveals.
articles.content:
When Netflix acquired Ben Affleck’s mysterious AI company InterPositive earlier this year, it took the industry by storm.
